description: To provide what is needed for life and growth—food, care, attention, or whatever sustains. 'Lumera' names the active giving that enables flourishing, whether feeding a child, watering a plant, or supporting a friend through difficulty. It is care made tangible through provision.
sound_symbolism: The word flows like sustenance itself. 'Lu' begins with liquid life-giving, nourishment flowing outward. Then 'me' meets the recipient with nasal warmth, care offered personally. Finally 'ra' reaches into growth, the nourished one strengthened.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia thia lumera.' (I nourish you.) The means can be marked with the instrumental: 'mia roe nuora thia lumera.' (I nourish you with food.) Related to 'pharelo' (cook) but broader—lumera includes all forms of sustaining provision.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Nourishing communities and ecosystems sustains life across generations. What we feed grows; what we starve withers.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Nourishing others practices generosity and loving-kindness—transforming resources into care, meeting need with response.
